finding: correctness pass: run_office_js round-trip, new Function async-body wrapping, serializeResult round-trip all verified sound; no defect
run_office_js (arbitrary write) routes via broker::call_pane which has a sole-pane fallback, so a single connected pane could receive a write whose doc_full_name does not match the model's target
rationale: NOT introduced here: add_comment/set_track_changes already route identically via broker::call_pane; the pane-side dispatchOp sameDoc guard rejects a mismatched target for any pane with a known doc key; the only residual (unsaved doc, empty SELF_DOC_KEY) is the deliberate pane-rpc DEC-1 sole-pane fallback. Pre-existing routing model, mitigated; out of this feature's scope.

finding: describeError does msg.indexOf(e.name) after msg=(e.message||String(e)); a thrown value with a truthy NON-string .message plus a .name makes msg a number and msg.indexOf a TypeError, which rejects the .catch with no replyErr sent -> hang until daemon OFFICE_PANE_TIMEOUT
opRunOfficeJs has no pane-side timeout; a synchronously non-terminating script (while(true)) wedges the pane JS thread until add-in reload
rationale: Inherent to executing arbitrary synchronous JS in a single-threaded WebView (no way to interrupt sync JS without a Worker, which cannot access the Office.js context). The daemon 15s timeout bounds the CALLER. run_office_js is user-approved arbitrary code by design. Documented as a known limitation in the handler comment.

finding: serializeResult 'NEVER throws' invariant is inaccurate: String(value) in the catch can re-throw if value.toString/valueOf throws (caught downstream by opRunOfficeJs .catch, so no crash)
serializeResult re-parses the just-stringified value (JSON.parse(c.text)) on the non-truncated path though the native value is already in hand
JSON.stringify(value) fully materializes before capText truncates (unbounded intermediate before the 100000 cap)
rationale: Inherent to measuring serialized size; the model's approved script must return a value and any size-based cap needs the serialized form first. Same pattern as read_document's capText.

finding: header doc comment has a stray unbalanced close paren in '{Word,Excel,PowerPoint}.run)' plus a redundant trailing 'via Office.js'

comment 'These three are host-agnostic (Word + Excel + PowerPoint)' sits above a match arm listing only two patterns (read_document | get_selection); run_office_js is a separate arm below, so 'three' is stale
use axum::http::StatusCode may be orphaned after removing act_on_document
rationale: FALSE POSITIVE: grep confirms StatusCode still has 6 uses in macos.rs (install_cert_trust etc.); no unused-import warning at cargo check.

finding: DISPATCH_METHOD import may be orphaned after removing com_call
rationale: FALSE POSITIVE: DISPATCH_METHOD is still used at windows.rs:294 (com_item flags DISPATCH_METHOD.0 | DISPATCH_PROPERTYGET.0).

finding: header comment retains the lifecycle tag '(ITEM-9 / run_office_js)' though ITEM-9 tags were scrubbed from the Rust dispatch/protocol/tools comments

the real-LLM test final assertion out.result.is_some()||out.text.is_some() is effectively always-true (reply always carries text and a result key), verifying nothing about whether the model's script had effect
the mock-pane run_office_js round-trip asserts only the echo shape, not the {result,truncated,text}->pane_tool_result mapping in a non-ignored test
rationale: Partially addressed: pane_tool_result mapping is already covered by test9 (read_document); adding a text-surfacing assertion to the run_office_js test to reinforce.

finding: the describeError test never asserts the error .name ('RichApi.Error') is surfaced nor the name-in-message dedup branch
open_excel_and_wait_for_pane binds fixed port 44300, colliding with test13_live_mac_pane_ops (also 44300) under multi-threaded --ignored
rationale: Inherent to the live #[ignore] tests: each drives ONE Excel + one manual ribbon click, so they are physically run one-at-a-time; test13 has the identical constraint and the file header already documents --test-threads=1. Adding an explicit doc note on the new tests.

finding: connected_pane_keys().next() grabs an arbitrary process-global registry entry and could latch onto a stale pane key from an earlier test
rationale: The live helper runs only under a separate --ignored invocation in a fresh process where no prior mock panes exist; mock-pane sockets unregister server-side on close. Theoretical for the actual run modes; matches test13's existing approach.

finding: run_office_js_dispatch_round_trip ends with pane.abort() which does not synchronously unregister the mock pane
rationale: Follows the established convention of every mock-pane test in this file (test6/8/9/12/16 all end with pane.abort()); server-side close-detection unregisters asynchronously. Consistent with the file's pattern, not a new defect.

finding: authz + api-contract pass: per-call approval preserved (no bypass list touched), inputSchema well-formed, tool count consistent, no dangling edit_document reference; no defect

api-contract pass: run_office_js inputSchema well-formed (doc_full_name+script required strings), MCP tool_result shape preserved via pane_tool_result, tool count consistent between tools.rs and settings_mcp_test.rs EXPECTED_TOOLS, no dangling edit_document schema reference; no defect
